summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering various operational updates, personnel matters, and financial items. Key highlights included acceptance of an approximately $196,000 ARPA First Responder Retention Incentive Grant providing $4,000 bonuses to eligible police and fire employees, approval of two indigent burials, hiring of two new full-time police officers, and acceptance of two firefighter/EMT resignations. The board also approved converting a township-owned parking lot to grass and discussed progress on the digital sign, generator repair, tornado siren issues, and Station 18 construction permitting.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (July 25–August 7, 2023) – passed (Hodges abstained)
Motion to approve unpaid leave of absence for zoning inspector Nolani Tonganon, August 21–25, 2023 – passed
Motion to remove township-owned parking lot at 6765 Goshen Road and convert to grass – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Officer Gary Roush to attend Red Dot site instructor training at OPOTA, Nov 1–3 at $400 registration – passed
Motion to accept resignation of firefighter/paramedic John Rose effective August 1, 2023 – passed
Motion to accept resignation of firefighter/EMT Lucas Croner effective August 1, 2023 – passed
Motion to approve indigent burial of Randy D. Dallas at $803 to Advantage Cremations – passed
Motion to approve indigent burial of Janice M. Lay at $803 to Advantage Cremations – passed
Motion to buy back two graves from James and Cheryl Newsom at $200 each ($400 total) – passed
Motion to accept ARPA First Responder Retention Incentive Grant and authorize Township Administrator to sign documents – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session per ORC 121.22 G1 – passed
Motion to hire Daniel Pangallo as full-time police officer contingent upon pre-employment process – passed
Motion to hire Megan Sickles as full-time police officer contingent upon pre-employment process – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 7:41 PM – passed
action_items: Director Meadows and Chief Rose to resolve concrete sloping issue at generator site
Chief Myers to follow up on tornado siren repair on Peggy Lane and update the board
Chief Myers' architect to contact state and county building department regarding Station 18 permit denial questions
Administrator Rose to resubmit ARPA grant paperwork adjusting for employees no longer employed and return excess funds to state
Chad (Service Director) and team to remove parking lot at 6765 Goshen Road and convert to grass
Service department to address recalls on both International trucks in coming weeks
Trustee Hauserman to attend Clermont County Township Association meeting at Union Township regarding property tax re-evaluation support
key_amounts: $400 registration cost for Officer Roush Red Dot training
$803 indigent burial cost for Randy D. Dallas
$803 indigent burial cost for Janice M. Lay
$400 total to buy back two cemetery graves from Newsoms ($200 each)
~$196,000 ARPA First Responder Retention Incentive Grant received
$4,000 bonus per eligible full-time police officer and firefighter from ARPA grant
Local government fund distribution negotiated up from 1.8% to target of 2.3% (possibly 2.1%)
Property tax increase request submitted to state not to exceed 24% (state originally suggested 43%)
notable_topics: Generator restored to admin building after one year
Digital sign installation at Goshen Road and SR 28 and upcoming gateway sign at 28 and Deerfield
Tornado siren on Peggy Lane still under repair
Station 18 construction permit denial and follow-up with county building department and state
ARPA First Responder Retention Incentive Grant acceptance (~$196,000)
Medic 19 insurance replacement – 550 chassis found, approximately one year from completion
Two firefighter/EMT resignations due to pay competitiveness issues
Hiring of two new full-time police officers (Daniel Pangallo and Megan Sickles)
Conversion of township-owned parking lot to grass lot
Two indigent burials and state reimbursement process
Local government fund five-year distribution formula successfully negotiated and approved by county commissioners July 27
Property tax re-evaluation advocacy at state level
Service department dump truck returned after accident repair
Ashley Patrick (medic injured in accident) expected back in 3-4 weeks
Cemetery grave buyback from Newsom family
attendees: Dan (resident from Villages of Belmont, referenced)
notes: Two indigent burials were for neighbors at the same trailer park who died one day apart, which was noted as unusual. Trustee Hodges abstained from the payment of bills vote. Executive session was held from approximately 6:59 PM to 7:39 PM to discuss employment matters. The board discussed difficulty retaining and recruiting firefighters/EMTs due to pay competitiveness with other departments. Trustee Hauserman discussed his role leading local government fund negotiations over the past year resulting in a five-year distribution formula approved by county commissioners on July 27.
title: BOT Meeting 8-8-23
